signals.title: USD/JPY — BoJ Intervention Risk + Carry
signals.text: USD/JPY at 160.33, trading above the 160.00 level that has previously triggered BoJ verbal warnings. Despite the recent BoJ hike, the yen remains weak, underscoring deeply entrenched bearish positioning. The 296bp Fed-BoJ OIS carry premium (SOFR 3.69% vs TONA 0.73%) continues to sustain the bid, while JPY COT LF net short −115,036 (week ending Jun 9) indicates a crowded carry trade. Intervention risk is elevated on a sustained break above 160.00. Watch for any official comments from MoF/BoJ officials.
evidence: Yen stays weak after BoJ hike as analysts eye intervention risk

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + Domestic Data
signals.text: NZD/USD COT LF net short −22,354 (week ending Jun 9) vs RBNZ hike bias (Hike prob: 40% priced for 8 Jul). Specs are fading a hawkish CB, creating a short-squeeze risk if the RBNZ delivers or signals a hike. This is compounded by a sharply narrowing Q1 current account deficit (-NZ$1.008B vs exp -NZ$0.967B), but offset by a drop in consumer confidence to its lowest since 2023. NZD/USD at 0.5829. A clear hawkish signal from the RBNZ or a sustained improvement in economic data is likely to trigger a crowded spec short unwind.

signals.title: EUR/USD — ECB Hike + Geopolitical Trade Tensions
signals.text: EUR/USD at 1.1616. The ECB is seen hiking one more time in September before rates plateau through 2027, with the ECB's first rate increase since 2023 largely anticipated. However, Chinese export flood puts EU tariff wall firmly on the G7 agenda, signaling potential trade tensions that is likely to weigh on EUR exports. The 176bp OIS carry differential (SOFR 3.69% vs €STR 1.93%) favors USD, structurally limiting EUR/USD upside. Watch for G7 statements on trade tariffs and the next ECB meeting (mkt: 94% hike priced) on 23 Jul.
evidence: One more ECB hike seen in September
Chinese export flood puts EU tariff wall on G7 agenda

signals.title: AUD/USD — Volatility Premium + Negative CESI
signals.text: AUD/USD IV at 22.2% vs HV30 at 7.9% — a significant +14.3% VRP above realized volatility, indicating strong hedging demand. This elevated options premium suggests market participants are pricing in higher future volatility despite a relatively neutral COT LF net long +38,092 (week ending Jun 9). The AUD CESI net -8 misses (data disappointing) supports AUD weakness bias. AUD/USD at 0.7065. Watch for a shift in economic data surprises to alleviate the negative CESI pressure.
